NCT04526665.name: ELATIVE
NCT04526665.group: UDCA-inadequate-response PBC, elafibranor 80 mg PO daily vs placebo (wk 52, biochemical response RR)

allOutcomes.title: Biochemical response at week 52 (primary, composite ALP <1.67x ULN + >=15% reduction + normal bilirubin) (RR)

NCT04526665.snippet: Source: Kowdley KV et al. NEJM 2024;390:795-805 (ELATIVE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 161 adults with PBC and inadequate response to or intolerance of UDCA (ALP >=1.67x ULN or total bilirubin >1x ULN but <2x ULN) randomized 2:1 double-blind to elafibranor 80 mg PO daily (n=108) or matched placebo (n=53), stratified by response/intolerance status, for 52 weeks. Most patients continued background UDCA.

evidence.label: Primary - Biochemical Response at Week 52
evidence.text: Biochemical response (composite: ALP <1.67x ULN with >=15% decrease from baseline AND total bilirubin <=ULN) at week 52: elafibranor 55/108 (51%) vs placebo 2/53 (4%); risk ratio 13.4 (95% CI 3.4-53.0; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - ALP Normalisation, Pruritus, Liver Stiffness
evidence.text: ALP normalisation at week 52: elafibranor 15% vs placebo 0% (P<0.001). 5D-itch scale change in patients with moderate-severe baseline pruritus: -1.8 vs -0.6 (P=0.02). Liver stiffness (FibroScan) trend favoured elafibranor. Safety: GI AEs more common (abdominal pain, diarrhoea); no hepatotoxicity signal; CK elevation occasional.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR stratified by response/intolerance. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - central laboratory for ALP/bilirubin. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria~* Males or females age of 18 to 75 years (inclusive)~* Definite or probable Primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) diagnosis~* Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) ≥ 1.67x upper limit of normal (ULN)~* Total bilirubin (TB) ≤ 2x ULN~* Ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) for at least 12 months (stable dose ≥ 3 months) prior to screening, or unable to tolerate UDCA treatment (no UDCA for ≥ 3 months) prior to screening (per country standard-of-care dosing)~* Must have PBC Worst Itch Numeric rating scal...

NCT04620733.name: RESPONSE
NCT04620733.group: UDCA-inadequate-response PBC, seladelpar 10 mg PO daily vs placebo (month 12, biochemical response RR)
NCT04620733.snippet: Source: Hirschfield GM et al. NEJM 2024;390:783-794 (RESPONSE).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 193 adults with PBC and inadequate response to or intolerance of UDCA randomized 2:1 double-blind to seladelpar 10 mg PO daily (n=130) or matched placebo (n=63) for 12 months. Most patients continued background UDCA.

evidence.label: Primary - Biochemical Response at Month 12
evidence.text: Biochemical response (composite: ALP <1.67x ULN with >=15% decrease AND total bilirubin <=ULN) at month 12: seladelpar 81/130 (62%) vs placebo 13/63 (20%); risk ratio 3.00 (95% CI 1.81-4.98; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - ALP Normalisation, Pruritus, Fatigue
evidence.text: ALP normalisation at month 12: seladelpar 25% vs placebo 0% (P<0.001). PBC-40 pruritus domain change: -3.2 vs -1.7 (P=0.005). Fatigue domain not significantly different. Safety: balanced between arms; no hepatotoxicity signal, no disproportionate myalgia.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - central lab for biomarkers. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Must have given written informed consent (signed and dated) and any authorizations required by local law.~* Male or female with a definitive diagnosis of primary biliary cholangitis (PBC).~* Ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) for the past 12 months (stable dose for >3 months prior to screening) or intolerant to UDCA (last dose of UDCA >3 months prior to screening).~* Laboratory parameters measured by the Central Laboratory at screening:~  * Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) ≥1.67×...

title: RapidMeta Hepatology | PPAR Modulators (Elafibranor, Seladelpar) in PBC Second-Line v1.1
